summary: Decompiled the SysDolphin baselib list module by adding src/sysdolphin/baselib/list.c implementations and expanding list.h with HSD_SList/HSD_DList allocation/list APIs. The PR moved list.o from asm to src in obj_files.mk, added list.o to e_files.mk, and renamed the previously labeled HSD_SListPrepend symbol to HSD_SListAllocAndPrepend across assembly callers because the routine allocates a node before prepending, analogous to HSD_SListAllocAndAppend. The author also introduced a duplicate static inline HSD_SListAlloc_inline to match callers where HSD_SListAlloc behavior was inlined.
what_changed: Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/list.c with HSD list allocation data, single-list allocation helpers, append/prepend list helpers, and remove logic.

Added global HSD_ObjAllocData instances slist_alloc_data and dlist_alloc_data and initialization via HSD_ListInitAllocData using HSD_ObjAllocInit with HSD_SList and HSD_DList sizes and alignment/count argument 4.
Added HSD_SListGetAllocData and HSD_DListGetAllocData accessors returning the corresponding allocation-data globals.
Implemented HSD_SListAlloc using HSD_ObjAlloc, NULL assertion against existing string labels for "list.c" and "list", memset to zero, and return of the allocated HSD_SList.
Added static inline HSD_SListAlloc_inline duplicating HSD_SListAlloc behavior so functions whose original code inlined allocation could still match.
Implemented HSD_SListAllocAndAppend and HSD_SListAllocAndPrepend as allocate-then-set-data wrappers around HSD_SListAppendList and HSD_SListPrependList.
Implemented HSD_SListAppendList with an assert on NULL next, insertion after an existing list node when list is non-NULL, and direct return of next when list is NULL.
Implemented HSD_SListPrependList with an assert on NULL prev, setting prev->next to list, and returning prev.
Implemented HSD_SListRemove to free a non-NULL node through HSD_ObjFree and return its next pointer, or return NULL for NULL input.
Expanded src/sysdolphin/baselib/list.h to include objalloc.h, define HSD_DList, and declare the list APIs.
Moved list.o in obj_files.mk from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/list.o to src/sysdolphin/baselib/list.o and added list.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk.
Renamed HSD_SListPrepend to HSD_SListAllocAndPrepend in asm/sysdolphin/baselib/list.s and updated callsites in bytecode.s, code_80391580.s, lobj.s, shadow.s, and include/functions.h comments.
smart_moves: Renaming HSD_SListPrepend to HSD_SListAllocAndPrepend captured the function's actual semantics: it calls the SList allocator before prepending, matching the naming pattern of HSD_SListAllocAndAppend.

Keeping a separate static inline HSD_SListAlloc_inline avoided forcing all allocation sites through a callable function when the original code inlined the allocation sequence, a practical matching tactic for small allocator helpers.

The header update introduced HSD_DList alongside HSD_SList even though most visible implementation work was SList-focused, preserving the module-level allocation data split between slist_alloc_data and dlist_alloc_data.
The build-system change was minimal and targeted: list.o was switched from asm to src while assembly references that only needed symbol-name updates were preserved.
decomp_lessons: For decompilation naming, prefer behavior-rich names over literal guessed names: a function that allocates and prepends should be named HSD_SListAllocAndPrepend rather than HSD_SListPrepend.
Small allocator routines may need both an exported function and an inline duplicate to match callsites where the compiler emitted the helper body inline.
When replacing an asm object with a C object, update obj_files.mk and, if relevant for scheduling, e_files.mk at the same time so the build selects the new source object.
Preserve existing rodata/string label references such as lbl_804D5F48, lbl_804D5F50, lbl_804D5F58, and lbl_804D5F60 when they are required for matching asserts and source-line numbers.
When a renamed function remains called from undecompiled assembly files, all assembly call labels and include/functions.h comments should be updated consistently to avoid stale symbol names.
naming_conventions: HSD_SListAllocAndPrepend was chosen over HSD_SListPrepend because the routine allocates a new HSD_SList node before prepending it.
HSD_SListAllocAndAppend and HSD_SListAllocAndPrepend form a paired naming convention for allocate-plus-link wrappers.
HSD_SListAppendList and HSD_SListPrependList describe operations on already allocated list nodes, contrasting with the AllocAnd* wrappers.
Allocator data globals use lowercase module/object names: slist_alloc_data and dlist_alloc_data.
The inline matching helper was named HSD_SListAlloc_inline, explicitly marking it as a duplicate inline version rather than a public API.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used static inline HSD_SListAlloc_inline to reproduce inlined allocation sequences in HSD_SListAllocAndAppend and HSD_SListAllocAndPrepend.
Kept extern declarations for existing string labels used by __assert rather than replacing them with new string literals, helping preserve rodata layout and assert-line matching.
Updated assembly call targets from HSD_SListPrepend to HSD_SListAllocAndPrepend without changing branch offsets or code bytes, reflecting a symbol rename rather than behavioral assembly edits.
Moved only list.o from asm to src in obj_files.mk, leaving other baselib objects as asm or src according to their current decomp status.
Added list.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED, indicating this source file required epilogue scheduling handling for matching in this build setup.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify HSD_SListAlloc_inline or remove the duplicate inline allocator.
Search for subsequent corrections to HSD_SListAppendList/HSD_SListPrependList parameter names or behavior.
Look for later decompilation of HSD_DList functions using dlist_alloc_data.
Search for other PRs that rename functions from action-only names to AllocAnd* names after discovering allocation side effects.
Check whether list.c later gained explicit prototypes or void parameter lists for functions declared with empty parentheses.
